title: Buttery Juicy Uruguayan Turkey Tacos
description: A quick, buttery and juicy weeknight taco featuring ground turkey cooked with classic Uruguayan seasonings, served on warm tortillas.
instructions: 1. In a medium skillet, melt 15 g of butter over medium heat.
2. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
3. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
4. Add the ground turkey, breaking it up with a wooden spoon; cook until browned and no longer pink, about 5-6 minutes.
5. Season the turkey with cumin, paprika, oregano, ¼ tsp salt, pepper, and the remaining 15 g butter; stir to coat and continue cooking for 2 more minutes to allow the butter to melt into the meat.
6. While the turkey cooks, heat a dry skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
7. Place each tortilla on the hot skillet and toast each side for 30 seconds until pliable and lightly browned; this can also be done in an air fryer at 200°C for 3 minutes.
8. Remove the warm tortillas and spoon a generous portion of the buttery turkey mixture onto each one.
9. Optional: garnish with fresh cilantro, a squeeze of lime, or a dollop of salsa verde for extra brightness.
10. Serve immediately with lime wedges on the side for squeezing; enjoy the buttery, juicy flavor of Uruguayan-inspired tacos.
